title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Compiler Case: Server Timeout Budget
summary: A Node server inherits defaults without understanding header, request, keep-alive, upgrade, idle-socket, HTTP/2, or graceful-close behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts server timeout budget increment in Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Server Timeout Budget · Theory Server Timeout Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3290 in a burst intake service near its event-loop and queue limits. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Server Timeout Budget contract for the supplied data.
